briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was discovered to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded banking.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network in 2026, signalling a major geopolitical shift away from US-dominated card rails.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking and credit analytics firm Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial services and alternative credit decisioning.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[RBC fined $4.25 million over incorrect credit card statements](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/rbc-fined-credit-card-statements-9.7249811?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's largest bank has been hit with a $4.25 million fine by regulators for issuing inaccurate credit card statements to customers, highlighting ongoing consumer protection enforcement in Canadian financial services.

- **[Float announces $85-million Series C round to support AI push](https://betakit.com/float-announces-85-million-series-c-round-to-support-ai-push/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian B2B fintech Float has raised an $85-million Series C to accelerate its artificial intelligence capabilities across its corporate spend and financial management platform.

- **[Restaurants call on courts to force XTM to repay missing funds](https://betakit.com/restaurants-call-on-courts-to-force-xtm-to-repay-missing-funds/)** — *BetaKit* — Restaurant operators are pursuing legal action against Canadian fintech XTM, demanding the repayment of funds they allege have gone missing from the payments platform.

- **[Why advocates say "inclusive succession" is a hidden market opportunity for Canada](https://betakit.com/why-advocates-say-inclusive-succession-is-a-hidden-market-opportunity-for-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — Advocates argue that transferring small business ownership to underrepresented groups represents a significant and largely untapped economic and financial inclusion opportunity for Canada.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Included for context)* Canada's early positioning against screen scraping and its push toward a formal open banking framework laid the regulatory groundwork still shaping Canadian fintech policy today.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Five Eyes alliance warns of potential AI-powered cyber threat "crises"](https://betakit.com/five-eyes-alliance-warns-of-potential-ai-powered-cyber-threat-crises/)** — *BetaKit* — The Five Eyes intelligence alliance, which includes Canada, has issued a stark warning about AI-enabled cyberattacks that could create crisis-level disruptions — a critical concern for Atlantic Canadian fintech startups and credit unions building digital infrastructure.

- **[Klue hack leads to customer data breach](https://betakit.com/klue-hack-leads-to-customer-data-breach/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian competitive intelligence platform Klue suffered a cyberattack resulting in a customer data breach, serving as a timely reminder of the cybersecurity risks facing Canadian tech and fintech companies of all sizes.
